title: Why Vinyl Sounds Warmer than Digital

analysis.object: T635: Why Vinyl Sounds Warmer than Digital depth analysis

digital_eml0.description: Digital audio: EML-0 discrete samples; quantized, integer-valued
digital_eml0.reason: Digital is EML-0: discrete 16/24-bit samples; no inter-sample information

vinyl_eml3.description: Vinyl: analog continuous signal with harmonic distortion adding EML-3 overtones
vinyl_eml3.reason: Vinyl is EML-3: continuous analog wave with EML-3 harmonic content from even-order distortion

warmth_is_depth3.description: Warmth is the subjective experience of EML-3 harmonic richness absent in EML-0 digital
warmth_is_depth3.reason: Vinyl vs digital debate is a depth classification argument: EML-3 warmth vs EML-0 precision

analysis.theorem: T635: Why Vinyl Sounds Warmer than Digital (S914).

key_theorem: T635: Why Vinyl Sounds Warmer than Digital (S914).
